_schema_notes: Per-state source of truth for open-records / open-meetings law.
Observers and records-request PDF generators read from this file instead
of hardcoding state-specific statute citations. Add a new state by adding
a top-level key with the same shape; the rest of the pipeline works without
open_records_act = the law governing public access to government records
open_meetings_act = the law governing what must be public about meetings
campaign_finance_act = the law governing candidate disclosure filings.
finding_categories = the specific statute each TownWatch finding cites.

minutes_missing.statute_text: Minutes of all meetings of public agencies shall be promptly recorded and shall be open to public inspection once approved as official by the agency.

agenda_missing.statute_text: The agenda of all matters expected to come before a public agency shall be made available, upon request, to the public for inspection as far in advance of the meeting as is reasonably possible.

campaign_finance_missing.statute_text: Candidates for municipal office shall file Campaign Contribution Disclosure Reports at the deadlines specified by the Act, including pre-election, post-election, and semi-annual reports.

member_roster_missing.statute_text: All public records shall be open for personal inspection and copying by any person at a reasonable time and place. The membership of a public agency — including appointed boards and commissions — is a public record.
